If for some reason the owner forgets to renew, it passes through certain stages and becomes available again to anyone who wishes to register it. I'm guessing this is what happened in this case. And that explains the direct traffic I've been getting on the name since I acquired it. Not to worry though — I'm willing to let this name go at a considerable price. You're free to make me an offer if you feel my price is on the high side. Visit the listing to own this name for your business. Best regards.

customer_message: We have the .net version — why do we need the .com?
reply: When people get back to their computer they are going to type in the .com and forget the .net. The more you market the .net, the more traffic the .com will get. The more you develop the .net, the more you will need the .com in the future.

customer_message: We own the .net — is the .com really that important?
reply: That is not a problem at all. But I must tell you — for the duration I have owned this domain it has received about 2 to 5 visitors a day. This may be due to users trying to find your .net site, thinking it is a .com. Owning the .com version of your domain will allow you to capture those visitors and expose them to your business. I will lower the asking price if you are interested. You can easily forward the .com to the .net for a traffic boost.

customer_message: How much traffic could this domain actually bring?
reply: There is a real chance to be number one on Google for the exact search term. According to many SEO specialists, the number one position gets about 30 to 40% of the total search volume. This means you'll get a significant number of clicks per month. This domain can help get you there faster than building organic authority from scratch.

While the domain could still provide a nice SEO boost, I actually see it as a great addition from a branding and geo-authority perspective as well. Owning the exact match .com of your core service carries a certain credibility and authority in the market. I appreciate your best offer to buy. Best regards.

customer_message: Why can't I transfer this to my registrar right away?
reply: Currently the domain is registered with GoDaddy and cannot be transferred to another registrar for a period of 60 days because I just acquired it through a backorder. GoDaddy has a 60-day latency period before you can transfer — this is standard with any registrar once the ownership of the domain changes. A temporary workaround would be to create a GoDaddy account if you do not already have one, and then transfer to your preferred registrar after 60 days. Once I initiate the transfer, GoDaddy will send you instructions on how to accept it. The transfer is instant.
